title: satureja cordata subsp. rodriguezii
intro: Satureja cordata subsp. rodriguezii, also known as the Rodriguez's savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and acting as an antioxidant. The Rodriguez's savory can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in cooking and as a flavoring agent in traditional medicine.
